meaning: vt.瀹炶,浠庝簨,鍛婂彂,璧疯瘔vi.鍛婂彂,璧疯瘔,浣滄瀵熷畼
examples: The police have decided not to prosecute because the evidence is not strong enough.
He has decided to prosecute her after careful consideration of all the relevant facts.
The Attorney General was aware of the political implications of his decision to prosecute.
There may be another way to prosecute suspects but that also raises issues, said Calo.
IMO's 1988 Rome convention, which provides an international legal framework to chase, prosecute and extradite pirates.
